core_concept: Sun, Moon, and Stars: Predictable Patterns
facts: The Sun rises in the east and sets in the west because Earth rotates from west to east, making the Sun appear to move across the sky.
The Moon changes shape in a repeating pattern called phases, from new moon to full moon and back, over about 29 days.
Day and night happen because Earth rotates, so the side facing the Sun has day while the side away from the Sun has night.
Stars are visible at night but not during the day because the Sun's bright light in the sky hides them from our view.
misconceptions: Students think the Sun moves across the sky because it is traveling around Earth, not because Earth is rotating.
Students believe the Moon's shape changes because clouds cover parts of it, not because of the changing angle of sunlight hitting the Moon.
